text.title: Serverless IaC and Templates Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team adopts CDK because 'it is the newest'; no AWS-only constraint; multi-cloud plan exists.
antipattern.fix: Reject; require explicit fit/cost rationale in the IaC ADR.

antipattern.symptom: Deploy script has `if [[ "$ENV" == prod ]]; then ...` blocks for memory / IAM / log level.
antipattern.root-cause: r2 violated; stage divergence in scripts.
antipattern.fix: Move per-stage overrides into samconfig.toml / tfvars / cdk.json; remove conditional shell logic.

antipattern.symptom: IAM role grants `s3:*` on `*` for an S3-thumbnailer function.
antipattern.fix: Scope to `s3:GetObject` + `s3:PutObject` on specific bucket ARNs; cfn-nag in CI.

antipattern.symptom: Console edit changed Lambda memory; deploy pipeline overwrites silently weeks later; cost incident follows.
antipattern.fix: Add drift detection job + alert; revoke console-edit IAM.
